verses.transliteration: kai ho diabolos ho planōn autous eblēthē eis tēn limnēn tou pyros kai theiou, hopou kai to thērion kai ho pseudoprophētēs, kai basanisthēsontai hēmeras kai nyktos eis tous aiōnas tōn aiōnōn.
verses.study_note: The unholy trinity reunited in judgment: dragon, beast, false prophet. εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων (literally "unto the ages of the ages") is the strongest biblical idiom for unending duration.
verses.doctrinal_note: The serpent of Eden meets the seed of the woman's heel (Gen 3:15) at the lake of fire. The promise of Genesis is consummated here.

verses.kjv_text: And I saw a great white throne, and him that sat on it, from whose face the earth and the heaven fled away; and there was found no place for them.
verses.original_text: Καὶ εἶδον θρόνον μέγαν λευκὸν καὶ τὸν καθήμενον ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν, οὗ ἀπὸ τοῦ προσώπου ἔφυγεν ἡ γῆ καὶ ὁ οὐρανός, καὶ τόπος οὐχ εὑρέθη αὐτοῖς.
verses.transliteration: Kai eidon thronon megan leukon kai ton kathēmenon ep' auton, hou apo tou prosōpou ephygen hē gē kai ho ouranos, kai topos ouch heurethē autois.
verses.study_note: Three adjectives in apposition: great (greatness of authority), white (purity of justice), throne (sovereignty). Heaven and earth flee — the first creation cannot bear the unveiled Judge.
verses.doctrinal_note: God the Father judges through the Son, to whom all judgment has been committed (John 5:22). The bench of the universe is neither vacant nor partisan.

verses.kjv_text: And I saw the dead, small and great, stand before God; and the books were opened: and another book was opened, which is the book of life: and the dead were judged out of those things which were written in the books, according to their works.
verses.original_text: καὶ εἶδον τοὺς νεκρούς, τοὺς μεγάλους καὶ τοὺς μικρούς, ἑστῶτας ἐνώπιον τοῦ θρόνου, καὶ βιβλία ἠνοίχθησαν· καὶ ἄλλο βιβλίον ἠνοίχθη, ὅ ἐστιν τῆς ζωῆς· καὶ ἐκρίθησαν οἱ νεκροὶ ἐκ τῶν γεγραμμένων ἐν τοῖς βιβλίοις κατὰ τὰ ἔργα αὐτῶν.
verses.transliteration: kai eidon tous nekrous, tous megalous kai tous mikrous, hestōtas enōpion tou thronou, kai biblia ēnoichthēsan; kai allo biblion ēnoichthē, ho estin tēs zōēs; kai ekrithēsan hoi nekroi ek tōn gegrammenōn en tois bibliois kata ta erga autōn.
verses.study_note: "Small and great" — every social rank. Two sets of records: books (βιβλία) of deeds and the Book (βιβλίον) of life. Judgment proceeds from both, "according to works."
verses.doctrinal_note: Salvation is by grace through faith, but judgment is according to works (Rom 2:6) — the works that grace produces. The Book of Life records redemption; the books record evidence.

verses.kjv_text: And the sea gave up the dead which were in it; and death and hell delivered up the dead which were in them: and they were judged every man according to their works.
verses.original_text: καὶ ἔδωκεν ἡ θάλασσα τοὺς νεκροὺς τοὺς ἐν αὐτῇ, καὶ ὁ θάνατος καὶ ὁ ᾅδης ἔδωκαν τοὺς νεκροὺς τοὺς ἐν αὐτοῖς, καὶ ἐκρίθησαν ἕκαστος κατὰ τὰ ἔργα αὐτῶν.
verses.transliteration: kai edōken hē thalassa tous nekrous tous en autē, kai ho thanatos kai ho hadēs edōkan tous nekrous tous en autois, kai ekrithēsan hekastos kata ta erga autōn.
verses.study_note: The sea, death (θάνατος), and Hades (ᾅδης) — three reservoirs of the dead — empty completely. Each (ἕκαστος) is judged individually; no one is lost in the crowd.
verses.doctrinal_note: No grave is too obscure, no body too dispersed, for resurrection. Christ holds the keys of death and Hades (1:18); the doors swing open at His word.

verses.kjv_text: And death and hell were cast into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
verses.original_text: καὶ ὁ θάνατος καὶ ὁ ᾅδης ἐβλήθησαν εἰς τὴν λίμνην τοῦ πυρός. οὗτος ὁ θάνατος ὁ δεύτερός ἐστιν, ἡ λίμνη τοῦ πυρός.
verses.transliteration: kai ho thanatos kai ho hadēs eblēthēsan eis tēn limnēn tou pyros. houtos ho thanatos ho deuteros estin, hē limnē tou pyros.
verses.study_note: Death itself dies (cf. 1 Cor 15:26). The lake of fire is identified as "the second death" — a defining gloss for the next chapter.
verses.doctrinal_note: For the saints, death has already been swallowed up in victory (1 Cor 15:54). The second death has no claim on those whose names are in the Lamb's Book of Life.

verses.kjv_text: And whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire.
verses.original_text: καὶ εἴ τις οὐχ εὑρέθη ἐν τῇ βίβλῳ τῆς ζωῆς γεγραμμένος, ἐβλήθη εἰς τὴν λίμνην τοῦ πυρός.
verses.transliteration: kai ei tis ouch heurethē en tē biblō tēs zōēs gegrammenos, eblēthē eis tēn limnēn tou pyros.
verses.study_note: The chapter ends not with a roar but a verdict. The conditional εἴ τις ("if anyone") leaves room for none to be skipped and none to be condemned apart from the open record.
verses.doctrinal_note: The decisive question for every soul is not deeds first, but whether the Lamb has written one's name. That writing is offered today to all who come to Christ in faith (John 6:37).

1.meaning: Bottomless pit; the deep — used in LXX of the primeval deep (Gen 1:2) and in Luke 8:31 of the demons' dread.
Chain; the same word for Paul's prison chain (Acts 28:20). The dragon is bound as a captive of war.

2.meaning: Dragon, sea-monster — recalls Leviathan (Isa 27:1) and the chaos beast crushed by God.
Serpent — directly tying this dragon to Eden (Gen 3:1). One enemy across the whole canon.

3.meaning: To set a seal upon — official imperial security; cf. the seal on Christ's tomb (Matt 27:66) which proved no obstacle to Him.

4.meaning: To behead with an axe (πέλεκυς); Roman execution reserved for capital crimes — the martyr's death.
To reign as king — the saints share Christ's royal office (cf. 5:10).

6.meaning: Priest — those who draw near to God on behalf of others; the Edenic and Sinaitic vocation finally realized.

8.meaning: To deceive, lead astray — Satan's defining verb across Revelation (12:9; 13:14; 19:20).

10.meaning: To torment, test by torture — used of testing metals on a touchstone (βάσανος). Eternal, not annihilating.

11.meaning: White — purity, victory, judicial impartiality; the same color as the Son of Man's hair (1:14).

12.meaning: Book, scroll — heaven's judicial record. Cf. the book of remembrance in Mal 3:16.
Work, deed — the visible fruit by which the inner faith is tested (Jas 2:18).

13.meaning: Hades, the realm of the dead — distinguished here from the lake of fire; Hades itself is destroyed.

14.meaning: Death — personified and finally destroyed (1 Cor 15:26). The last enemy meets its end.

15.meaning: Life — particularly the eternal life rooted in God; written in the Lamb's book before the foundation of the world (13:8).

meta.overview: After the dissolution of the first creation, John sees a new heaven and a new earth, and the holy city, New Jerusalem, descending from God. God Himself dwells with His people. Tears, death, sorrow, crying, and pain are abolished. The Bride-City is measured: a luminous cube of jasper and gold, twelve gates of pearl named for the tribes of Israel, twelve foundations named for the apostles of the Lamb. There is no temple, for the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are its temple; no sun, for the glory of God is its light. Nothing unclean enters; only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life.
meta.theme: All Things New: The Bride-City Comes Down from God
meta.keyWordMeaning: New (kainos) — qualitatively new, not merely chronologically more recent. A renewed creation of unprecedented kind.
outline: 1-8 New heaven and new earth; God dwells with men
9-14 The holy city — its wall, gates, and foundations
15-21 Measurements and materials of New Jerusalem
22-27 No temple, no sun, no night, no defilement

verses.kjv_text: And I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth were passed away; and there was no more sea.
verses.original_text: Καὶ εἶδον οὐρανὸν καινὸν καὶ γῆν καινήν· ὁ γὰρ πρῶτος οὐρανὸς καὶ ἡ πρώτη γῆ ἀπῆλθαν, καὶ ἡ θάλασσα οὐκ ἔστιν ἔτι.
verses.transliteration: Kai eidon ouranon kainon kai gēn kainēn; ho gar prōtos ouranos kai hē prōtē gē apēlthan, kai hē thalassa ouk estin eti.
verses.study_note: καινός (qualitatively new), not νέος (chronologically new). The vision echoes Isa 65:17; 66:22. The "no more sea" recalls Revelation's sea as origin of the beast (13:1) and reservoir of the dead (20:13).
verses.doctrinal_note: Creation is not annihilated but renewed — paralleling the resurrection of the body. The Christian hope is not escape from matter but its glorification.

verses.kjv_text: And I John saw the holy city, new Jerusalem, coming down from God out of heaven, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
verses.original_text: καὶ τὴν πόλιν τὴν ἁγίαν Ἰερουσαλὴμ καινὴν εἶδον καταβαίνουσαν ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ ἀπὸ τοῦ Θεοῦ, ἡτοιμασμένην ὡς νύμφην κεκοσμημένην τῷ ἀνδρὶ αὐτῆς.
verses.transliteration: kai tēn polin tēn hagian Ierousalēm kainēn eidon katabainousan ek tou ouranou apo tou Theou, hētoimasmenēn hōs nymphēn kekosmēmenēn tō andri autēs.
verses.study_note: The city descends — heaven comes to earth, not vice versa. Two metaphors fuse: city (πόλις, civic order) and bride (νύμφη, intimate love). Both are perfectly prepared (ἡτοιμασμένην).
verses.doctrinal_note: The dwelling of God with man is not a private mystic union but a corporate, public, civic reality — a city. Salvation is communal, not solitary.

verses.kjv_text: And I heard a great voice out of heaven saying, Behold, the tabernacle of God is with men, and he will dwell with them, and they shall be his people, and God himself shall be with them, and be their God.
verses.original_text: καὶ ἤκουσα φωνῆς μεγάλης ἐκ τοῦ θρόνου λεγούσης, Ἰδοὺ ἡ σκηνὴ τοῦ Θεοῦ μετὰ τῶν ἀνθρώπων, καὶ σκηνώσει μετ᾽ αὐτῶν, καὶ αὐτοὶ λαοὶ αὐτοῦ ἔσονται, καὶ αὐτὸς ὁ Θεὸς μετ᾽ αὐτῶν ἔσται, αὐτῶν Θεός.
verses.transliteration: kai ēkousa phōnēs megalēs ek tou thronou legousēs, Idou hē skēnē tou Theou meta tōn anthrōpōn, kai skēnōsei met' autōn, kai autoi laoi autou esontai, kai autos ho Theos met' autōn estai, autōn Theos.
verses.study_note: σκηνή ("tabernacle") and σκηνώσει ("shall dwell") evoke the wilderness tent and John 1:14 ("the Word became flesh and tabernacled"). The covenant formula "they shall be my people, I will be their God" (Ex 6:7; Jer 31:33) reaches its zenith.
verses.doctrinal_note: Every covenant from Eden to Sinai to Calvary aimed at this one goal: God with us. Immanuel is not only Christ's name but the consummation of redemption.

verses.kjv_text: And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain: for the former things are passed away.
verses.original_text: καὶ ἐξαλείψει πᾶν δάκρυον ἐκ τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν αὐτῶν, καὶ ὁ θάνατος οὐκ ἔσται ἔτι, οὔτε πένθος οὔτε κραυγὴ οὔτε πόνος οὐκ ἔσται ἔτι, ὅτι τὰ πρῶτα ἀπῆλθαν.
verses.transliteration: kai exaleipsei pan dakryon ek tōn ophthalmōn autōn, kai ho thanatos ouk estai eti, oute penthos oute kraugē oute ponos ouk estai eti, hoti ta prōta apēlthan.
verses.study_note: Five "no mores": tears, death, mourning, crying, pain. The verb ἐξαλείψει ("shall wipe out") is tender and personal — God's own hand. πένθος is the grief of bereavement; κραυγή is the cry of distress; πόνος is the labor of pain.
verses.doctrinal_note: Heaven includes the wiping away of tears — implying that some tears arrive there with us. The Comforter completes His ministry face to face.

verses.kjv_text: And he that sat upon the throne said, Behold, I make all things new. And he said unto me, Write: for these words are true and faithful.
verses.original_text: καὶ εἶπεν ὁ καθήμενος ἐπὶ τῷ θρόνῳ, Ἰδοὺ καινὰ ποιῶ πάντα. καὶ λέγει, Γράψον, ὅτι οὗτοι οἱ λόγοι πιστοὶ καὶ ἀληθινοί εἰσιν.
verses.transliteration: kai eipen ho kathēmenos epi tō thronō, Idou kaina poiō panta. kai legei, Grapson, hoti houtoi hoi logoi pistoi kai alēthinoi eisin.
verses.study_note: God speaks directly in Revelation only twice (1:8; here). καινὰ ποιῶ πάντα — "I make all things new" — is the divine slogan of restoration. πιστοὶ καὶ ἀληθινοί ("faithful and true") are also Christ's titles (19:11).
verses.doctrinal_note: God does not make all new things; He makes all things new. Redemption restores and elevates the original creation rather than discarding it.

verses.kjv_text: And he said unto me, It is done. I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will give unto him that is athirst of the fountain of the water of life freely.
verses.original_text: καὶ εἶπέν μοι, Γέγοναν. ἐγώ εἰμι τὸ Ἄλφα καὶ τὸ Ὦ, ἡ ἀρχὴ καὶ τὸ τέλος. ἐγὼ τῷ διψῶντι δώσω ἐκ τῆς πηγῆς τοῦ ὕδατος τῆς ζωῆς δωρεάν.
verses.transliteration: kai eipen moi, Gegonan. egō eimi to Alpha kai to Ō, hē archē kai to telos. egō tō dipsōnti dōsō ek tēs pēgēs tou hydatos tēs zōēs dōrean.
verses.study_note: γέγοναν — perfect tense, "they have come to pass." The Alpha and Omega title ties this to the opening of the book (1:8). δωρεάν ("freely") closes the gap between thirst and supply: the gift is purely of grace.
verses.doctrinal_note: The gospel offer is not retracted at the end. The very last picture of God in the Bible is of One offering free water to thirsty souls.

verses.kjv_text: He that overcometh shall inherit all things; and I will be his God, and he shall be my son.
verses.original_text: ὁ νικῶν κληρονομήσει ταῦτα, καὶ ἔσομαι αὐτῷ Θεὸς καὶ αὐτὸς ἔσται μοι υἱός.
verses.transliteration: ho nikōn klēronomēsei tauta, kai esomai autō Theos kai autos estai moi huios.
verses.study_note: ὁ νικῶν ("the one overcoming") — the recurring promise from chapters 2–3, finally redeemed. The Davidic adoption formula (2 Sam 7:14) is here universalized to every believer.
verses.doctrinal_note: Justification by faith and adoption are inseparable: those declared righteous are also made sons, with full inheritance rights (Rom 8:17).

verses.kjv_text: But the fearful, and unbelieving, and the abominable, and murderers, and whoremongers, and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all liars, shall have their part in the lake which burneth with fire and brimstone: which is the second death.
verses.original_text: τοῖς δὲ δειλοῖς καὶ ἀπίστοις καὶ ἐβδελυγμένοις καὶ φονεῦσιν καὶ πόρνοις καὶ φαρμάκοις καὶ εἰδωλολάτραις καὶ πᾶσιν τοῖς ψευδέσιν, τὸ μέρος αὐτῶν ἐν τῇ λίμνῃ τῇ καιομένῃ πυρὶ καὶ θείῳ, ὅ ἐστιν ὁ θάνατος ὁ δεύτερος.
verses.transliteration: tois de deilois kai apistois kai ebdelygmenois kai phoneusin kai pornois kai pharmakois kai eidōlolatrais kai pasin tois pseudesin, to meros autōn en tē limnē tē kaiomenē pyri kai theiō, ho estin ho thanatos ho deuteros.
verses.study_note: Eight categories of the excluded. δειλοί ("cowards") leads — the apostates who denied Christ under pressure. φάρμακος (sorcerer) gives English "pharmacy" — the dark arts of pagan religion.
verses.doctrinal_note: The reverse side of beatitude is solemn warning. Those who continue unrepentantly in these sins prove that they are not new creations and therefore have no place in the new creation.

verses.kjv_text: And there came unto me one of the seven angels which had the seven vials full of the seven last plagues, and talked with me, saying, Come hither, I will shew thee the bride, the Lamb's wife.
verses.original_text: Καὶ ἦλθεν εἷς ἐκ τῶν ἑπτὰ ἀγγέλων τῶν ἐχόντων τὰς ἑπτὰ φιάλας τῶν γεμόντων τῶν ἑπτὰ πληγῶν τῶν ἐσχάτων, καὶ ἐλάλησεν μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ λέγων, Δεῦρο, δείξω σοι τὴν νύμφην τὴν γυναῖκα τοῦ Ἀρνίου.
verses.transliteration: Kai ēlthen heis ek tōn hepta angelōn tōn echontōn tas hepta phialas tōn gemontōn tōn hepta plēgōn tōn eschatōn, kai elalēsen met' emou legōn, Deuro, deixō soi tēn nymphēn tēn gynaika tou Arniou.
verses.study_note: Deliberate parallel with 17:1: a bowl-angel showed John the harlot; a bowl-angel now shows him the bride. Two cities, two women, two destinies.
verses.doctrinal_note: God's purpose for the redeemed community is intimate union with Christ — not service alone, but marriage.

verses.kjv_text: And he carried me away in the spirit to a great and high mountain, and shewed me that great city, the holy Jerusalem, descending out of heaven from God,
verses.original_text: καὶ ἀπήνεγκέν με ἐν πνεύματι ἐπὶ ὄρος μέγα καὶ ὑψηλόν, καὶ ἔδειξέν μοι τὴν πόλιν τὴν ἁγίαν Ἰερουσαλὴμ καταβαίνουσαν ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ ἀπὸ τοῦ Θεοῦ,
verses.transliteration: kai apēnenken me en pneumati epi oros mega kai hypsēlon, kai edeixen moi tēn polin tēn hagian Ierousalēm katabainousan ek tou ouranou apo tou Theou,
verses.study_note: Mountain visions echo Ezek 40:2. ἐν πνεύματι ("in the Spirit") marks Revelation's great vision-points (1:10; 4:2; 17:3; 21:10).
verses.doctrinal_note: Revelation, the most pneumatic book of the canon, comes to those who are themselves "in the Spirit." Spiritual sight is the indispensable pre-condition of theological vision.

verses.kjv_text: Having the glory of God: and her light was like unto a stone most precious, even like a jasper stone, clear as crystal;
verses.original_text: ἔχουσαν τὴν δόξαν τοῦ Θεοῦ· ὁ φωστὴρ αὐτῆς ὅμοιος λίθῳ τιμιωτάτῳ, ὡς λίθῳ ἰάσπιδι κρυσταλλίζοντι·
verses.transliteration: echousan tēn doxan tou Theou; ho phōstēr autēs homoios lithō timiōtatō, hōs lithō iaspidi krystallizonti;
verses.study_note: φωστήρ — luminary, lamp; cf. Gen 1:14 (LXX) of sun and moon. The city's light is not its own; it shines because God's glory is in it. ἴασπις (jasper) was the first stone in 4:3, around the throne.
verses.doctrinal_note: The church is light only as it reflects God's glory (cf. 2 Cor 3:18). The brightness of the new creation is borrowed light, but no less real for that.

verses.kjv_text: And had a wall great and high, and had twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels, and names written thereon, which are the names of the twelve tribes of the children of Israel:
verses.original_text: ἔχουσα τεῖχος μέγα καὶ ὑψηλόν, ἔχουσα πυλῶνας δώδεκα, καὶ ἐπὶ τοῖς πυλῶσιν ἀγγέλους δώδεκα, καὶ ὀνόματα ἐπιγεγραμμένα, ἅ ἐστιν τὰ ὀνόματα τῶν δώδεκα φυλῶν τῶν υἱῶν Ἰσραήλ.
verses.transliteration: echousa teichos mega kai hypsēlon, echousa pylōnas dōdeka, kai epi tois pylōsin angelous dōdeka, kai onomata epigegrammena, ha estin ta onomata tōn dōdeka phylōn tōn huiōn Israēl.
verses.study_note: Twelve gates, twelve angel-guardians, twelve tribal names. The gates are not for defense but for ceremony; in v. 25 they are never shut.
verses.doctrinal_note: The promises to Israel are not erased in the eschaton; they are inscribed on the very gates of the city. There is one people of God, and Israel's tribes are foundational to its access.

verses.kjv_text: On the east three gates; on the north three gates; on the south three gates; and on the west three gates.
verses.original_text: ἀπὸ ἀνατολῆς πυλῶνες τρεῖς, καὶ ἀπὸ βορρᾶ πυλῶνες τρεῖς, καὶ ἀπὸ νότου πυλῶνες τρεῖς, καὶ ἀπὸ δυσμῶν πυλῶνες τρεῖς.
verses.transliteration: apo anatolēs pylōnes treis, kai apo borra pylōnes treis, kai apo notou pylōnes treis, kai apo dysmōn pylōnes treis.
verses.study_note: The arrangement matches the wilderness camp (Num 2) and Ezekiel's ideal city (Ezek 48:30–34). Access from every direction signals a worldwide gathering.
verses.doctrinal_note: No quarter of the world is too distant. From east, north, south, and west the redeemed enter (Luke 13:29).

verses.kjv_text: And the wall of the city had twelve foundations, and in them the names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
verses.original_text: καὶ τὸ τεῖχος τῆς πόλεως ἔχων θεμελίους δώδεκα, καὶ ἐπ᾽ αὐτῶν δώδεκα ὀνόματα τῶν δώδεκα ἀποστόλων τοῦ Ἀρνίου.
verses.transliteration: kai to teichos tēs poleōs echōn themelious dōdeka, kai ep' autōn dōdeka onomata tōn dōdeka apostolōn tou Arniou.
verses.study_note: Twelve apostles — the New Testament counterpart of the twelve tribes. Together they witness one continuous people of God across both covenants (cf. Eph 2:20).
verses.doctrinal_note: The church is built on apostolic foundation — that is, on the apostles' Christ-given testimony, now fixed for all time in the New Testament Scriptures.

verses.kjv_text: And he that talked with me had a golden reed to measure the city, and the gates thereof, and the wall thereof.
verses.original_text: Καὶ ὁ λαλῶν μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ εἶχεν μέτρον κάλαμον χρυσοῦν, ἵνα μετρήσῃ τὴν πόλιν καὶ τοὺς πυλῶνας αὐτῆς καὶ τὸ τεῖχος αὐτῆς.
verses.transliteration: Kai ho lalōn met' emou eichen metron kalamon chrysoun, hina metrēsē tēn polin kai tous pylōnas autēs kai to teichos autēs.
verses.study_note: A golden reed (κάλαμος) — measurement is an act of consecration (cf. Ezek 40:3; Rev 11:1). The reed is gold to match the city.
verses.doctrinal_note: God measures what He owns. The new Jerusalem is precise, not vague — every dimension is divinely fixed.

verses.kjv_text: And the city lieth foursquare, and the length is as large as the breadth: and he measured the city with the reed, twelve thousand furlongs. The length and the breadth and the height of it are equal.
verses.original_text: καὶ ἡ πόλις τετράγωνος κεῖται, καὶ τὸ μῆκος αὐτῆς ὅσον τὸ πλάτος. καὶ ἐμέτρησεν τὴν πόλιν τῷ καλάμῳ ἐπὶ σταδίων δώδεκα χιλιάδων· τὸ μῆκος καὶ τὸ πλάτος καὶ τὸ ὕψος αὐτῆς ἴσα ἐστίν.
verses.transliteration: kai hē polis tetragōnos keitai, kai to mēkos autēs hoson to platos. kai emetrēsen tēn polin tō kalamō epi stadiōn dōdeka chiliadōn; to mēkos kai to platos kai to hypsos autēs isa estin.
verses.study_note: τετράγωνος ("four-square"); the cube shape (length = width = height) was the form of the holy of holies in the temple (1 Kgs 6:20). The whole city is now most-holy place. 12,000 furlongs (στάδιον) ≈ 1,400 miles per side.
verses.doctrinal_note: The whole redeemed community lives in the immediate presence of God. The veil is gone; the holy of holies is everywhere.

verses.kjv_text: And he measured the wall thereof, an hundred and forty and four cubits, according to the measure of a man, that is, of the angel.
verses.original_text: καὶ ἐμέτρησεν τὸ τεῖχος αὐτῆς ἑκατὸν τεσσεράκοντα τεσσάρων πηχῶν, μέτρον ἀνθρώπου, ὅ ἐστιν ἀγγέλου.
verses.transliteration: kai emetrēsen to teichos autēs hekaton tesserakonta tessarōn pēchōn, metron anthrōpou, ho estin angelou.
verses.study_note: 144 (= 12 × 12) cubits — a symbolic number embracing both Israel's tribes and Christ's apostles. "Of a man, that is, of the angel" — human measurement is also angelic in this realm; the wall, by city standards, is modest.
verses.doctrinal_note: Symbolism speaks where literal proportion fails. The wall is for identity (insiders/outsiders) more than defense.

verses.kjv_text: And the building of the wall of it was of jasper: and the city was pure gold, like unto clear glass.
verses.original_text: καὶ ἡ ἐνδώμησις τοῦ τείχους αὐτῆς ἴασπις, καὶ ἡ πόλις χρυσίον καθαρὸν ὅμοιον ὑάλῳ καθαρῷ.
verses.transliteration: kai hē endōmēsis tou teichous autēs iaspis, kai hē polis chrysion katharon homoion hyalō katharō.
verses.study_note: ἐνδώμησις ("building material," masonry). Gold "like clear glass" — refined to translucency, no longer opaque. Glory fills every street.
verses.doctrinal_note: Earthly riches reach their highest use as building material for God's presence. What men hoard, God paves with.

verses.kjv_text: And the foundations of the wall of the city were garnished with all manner of precious stones. The first foundation was jasper; the second, sapphire; the third, a chalcedony; the fourth, an emerald;
verses.original_text: οἱ θεμέλιοι τοῦ τείχους τῆς πόλεως παντὶ λίθῳ τιμίῳ κεκοσμημένοι· ὁ θεμέλιος ὁ πρῶτος ἴασπις, ὁ δεύτερος σάπφειρος, ὁ τρίτος χαλκηδών, ὁ τέταρτος σμάραγδος,
verses.transliteration: hoi themelioi tou teichous tēs poleōs panti lithō timiō kekosmēmenoi; ho themelios ho prōtos iaspis, ho deuteros sappheiros, ho tritos chalkēdōn, ho tetartos smaragdos,
verses.study_note: The list parallels the high priest's breastplate (Ex 28:17–20), declaring the city itself a priestly garment. Twelve gemstones for twelve apostles.
verses.doctrinal_note: The church carries on the priestly representation of God's people, mediating worship in the new creation.

verses.kjv_text: The fifth, sardonyx; the sixth, sardius; the seventh, chrysolyte; the eighth, beryl; the ninth, a topaz; the tenth, a chrysoprasus; the eleventh, a jacinth; the twelfth, an amethyst.
verses.original_text: ὁ πέμπτος σαρδόνυξ, ὁ ἕκτος σάρδιον, ὁ ἕβδομος χρυσόλιθος, ὁ ὄγδοος βήρυλλος, ὁ ἔνατος τοπάζιον, ὁ δέκατος χρυσόπρασος, ὁ ἑνδέκατος ὑάκινθος, ὁ δωδέκατος ἀμέθυστος.
verses.transliteration: ho pemptos sardonyx, ho hektos sardion, ho hebdomos chrysolithos, ho ogdoos bēryllos, ho enatos topazion, ho dekatos chrysoprasos, ho hendekatos hyakinthos, ho dōdekatos amethystos.
verses.study_note: The twelve gems span the spectrum of color and refraction — green, blue, red, gold, violet — making the city a prismatic kaleidoscope of glory.
verses.doctrinal_note: Beauty is a divine attribute. The new creation is not utilitarian but stunningly aesthetic; the redeemed live in artistry as well as truth.

verses.kjv_text: And the twelve gates were twelve pearls; every several gate was of one pearl: and the street of the city was pure gold, as it were transparent glass.
verses.original_text: καὶ οἱ δώδεκα πυλῶνες δώδεκα μαργαρῖται, ἀνὰ εἷς ἕκαστος τῶν πυλώνων ἦν ἐξ ἑνὸς μαργαρίτου. καὶ ἡ πλατεῖα τῆς πόλεως χρυσίον καθαρὸν ὡς ὕαλος διαυγής.
verses.transliteration: kai hoi dōdeka pylōnes dōdeka margaritai, ana heis hekastos tōn pylōnōn ēn ex henos margaritou. kai hē plateia tēs poleōs chrysion katharon hōs hyalos diaugēs.
verses.study_note: A single pearl per gate — pearls of impossible scale. Pearls form by an oyster's response to wounding; the gates of glory bear the marks of suffering transformed.
verses.doctrinal_note: Entry to the kingdom is through pearl — through wounds, ultimately Christ's wounds (Isa 53:5). There is no other gate.

verses.kjv_text: And I saw no temple therein: for the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are the temple of it.
verses.original_text: Καὶ ναὸν οὐκ εἶδον ἐν αὐτῇ, ὁ γὰρ Κύριος ὁ Θεὸς ὁ παντοκράτωρ ναὸς αὐτῆς ἐστιν, καὶ τὸ Ἀρνίον.
verses.transliteration: Kai naon ouk eidon en autē, ho gar Kyrios ho Theos ho pantokratōr naos autēs estin, kai to Arnion.
verses.study_note: A startling absence: no temple. ναός (sanctuary, holy place) is not needed because the city's entire reality is communion with God. Father and Lamb are spoken of with a single noun ναός — a profound affirmation of the deity of Christ.
verses.doctrinal_note: All temples — Solomonic, Herodian, even the church's building — are scaffolding. When God dwells with His people unmediated, scaffolding falls away.

verses.kjv_text: And the city had no need of the sun, neither of the moon, to shine in it: for the glory of God did lighten it, and the Lamb is the light thereof.
verses.original_text: καὶ ἡ πόλις οὐ χρείαν ἔχει τοῦ ἡλίου οὐδὲ τῆς σελήνης, ἵνα φαίνωσιν αὐτῇ· ἡ γὰρ δόξα τοῦ Θεοῦ ἐφώτισεν αὐτήν, καὶ ὁ λύχνος αὐτῆς τὸ Ἀρνίον.
verses.transliteration: kai hē polis ou chreian echei tou hēliou oude tēs selēnēs, hina phainōsin autē; hē gar doxa tou Theou ephōtisen autēn, kai ho lychnos autēs to Arnion.
verses.study_note: Echoes Isa 60:19. The Lamb is the λύχνος (lamp) holding the divine light. Christ does not compete with the Father's glory; He embodies and irradiates it.
verses.doctrinal_note: Christ is presented as the divine Lamp — the chosen and proper bearer of God's glory. The Trinitarian relations are shown in the very architecture of light.

verses.kjv_text: And the nations of them which are saved shall walk in the light of it: and the kings of the earth do bring their glory and honour into it.
verses.original_text: καὶ περιπατήσουσιν τὰ ἔθνη διὰ τοῦ φωτὸς αὐτῆς· καὶ οἱ βασιλεῖς τῆς γῆς φέρουσιν τὴν δόξαν αὐτῶν εἰς αὐτήν.
verses.transliteration: kai peripatēsousin ta ethnē dia tou phōtos autēs; kai hoi basileis tēs gēs pherousin tēn doxan autōn eis autēn.
verses.study_note: Nations (ἔθνη) and their kings — categories that throughout Revelation oppose the Lamb — here process in worship (Isa 60:3). The redeemed cultural achievements of humanity are brought in.
verses.doctrinal_note: Heaven includes culture redeemed. The genuine "glory" of the nations — art, science, music, justice — is purified and offered to God, not destroyed.

verses.kjv_text: And the gates of it shall not be shut at all by day: for there shall be no night there.
verses.original_text: καὶ οἱ πυλῶνες αὐτῆς οὐ μὴ κλεισθῶσιν ἡμέρας, νὺξ γὰρ οὐκ ἔσται ἐκεῖ.
verses.transliteration: kai hoi pylōnes autēs ou mē kleisthōsin hēmeras, nyx gar ouk estai ekei.
verses.study_note: Open gates would have signaled an unimaginable peace in the ancient city; the absence of night makes them open continuously. Cf. Isa 60:11.
verses.doctrinal_note: Perfect security and perfect access coexist. The kingdom is not a fortress against love.

verses.kjv_text: And they shall bring the glory and honour of the nations into it.
verses.original_text: καὶ οἴσουσιν τὴν δόξαν καὶ τὴν τιμὴν τῶν ἐθνῶν εἰς αὐτήν.
verses.transliteration: kai oisousin tēn doxan kai tēn timēn tōn ethnōn eis autēn.
verses.study_note: Repetition for emphasis. δόξαν καὶ τιμὴν (glory and honour) is a hendiadys for "all that is most precious." All earthly tribute is now Godward.
verses.doctrinal_note: Mission's ultimate end is doxology — every nation bringing its best to the throne (Ps 22:27).

verses.kjv_text: And there shall in no wise enter into it any thing that defileth, neither whatsoever worketh abomination, or maketh a lie: but they which are written in the Lamb's book of life.
verses.original_text: καὶ οὐ μὴ εἰσέλθῃ εἰς αὐτὴν πᾶν κοινὸν καὶ ποιῶν βδέλυγμα καὶ ψεῦδος, εἰ μὴ οἱ γεγραμμένοι ἐν τῷ βιβλίῳ τῆς ζωῆς τοῦ Ἀρνίου.
verses.transliteration: kai ou mē eiselthē eis autēn pan koinon kai poiōn bdelygma kai pseudos, ei mē hoi gegrammenoi en tō bibliō tēs zōēs tou Arniou.
verses.study_note: Triple exclusion: the common (κοινόν), the abominable (βδέλυγμα), the false (ψεῦδος). Triple double negative (οὐ μὴ … εἰ μὴ) — strongest form of denial. Entry is restricted to those in the Lamb's book.
verses.doctrinal_note: Holiness is not optional in glory. The new Jerusalem is a holy city (v. 2); only those clothed in Christ's righteousness — recorded in His book — enter.

1.meaning: New in kind, qualitatively unprecedented (vs. νέος, merely new in time). Used of the new covenant, new commandment, new creation.

2.meaning: Bride; the church as the corporate Bride of Christ (cf. 19:7; Eph 5:25–27).

3.meaning: Tent, tabernacle — the wilderness dwelling of God; the same root as the verb in John 1:14 ("the Word was made flesh and dwelt").

4.meaning: To wipe out, blot out; used of God blotting out sins (Acts 3:19) and now of wiping every tear.

5.meaning: Faithful, trustworthy — Christ's own title (3:14; 19:11) and the warrant for our hope.

6.meaning: Freely, as a gift, without cost — emphatic adverb for grace; cf. Rom 3:24 ("justified freely").

7.meaning: To inherit; the legal language of full sonship (Rom 8:17).

8.meaning: Cowardly, timid — those who shrink back from confessing Christ (Mark 4:40; 2 Tim 1:7).
Sorcerer, dealer in magical drugs — the dark arts of pagan religion.

10.meaning: Spirit; "in the Spirit" marks Revelation's major vision-points (1:10; 4:2; 17:3; 21:10).

11.meaning: Luminary, source of light — used in Gen 1:14 LXX of sun and moon; here applied to the city.

16.meaning: Four-square; combined with equal height makes a perfect cube — the shape of the holy of holies (1 Kgs 6:20).

19.meaning: Foundation — Christ Himself is foundation (1 Cor 3:11), and the apostles are foundation in derivative sense (Eph 2:20).

21.meaning: Pearl — the costly pearl of the kingdom (Matt 13:46); formed by suffering, here become a gate of glory.

22.meaning: Sanctuary, inner shrine — distinguished from ἱερόν (temple precinct). The Lord and the Lamb together are this inner shrine.
Almighty, ruler of all — Revelation's favorite title for God (used 9 times).

23.meaning: Lamp — a vessel that holds and dispenses light. The Lamb holds and dispenses the Father's glory.

27.meaning: Abomination, detestable thing — what is utterly repugnant to God (cf. Daniel's "abomination of desolation").

meta.overview: The vision climaxes with a river of water of life flowing from the throne of God and of the Lamb, and the tree of life — Eden restored and amplified. The servants of God see His face; His name is on their foreheads; they reign forever. Christ confirms the trustworthiness of the vision and announces His imminent return. He warns against adding to or taking from the prophecy. The Spirit and the Bride join in the final invitation: "Come." The Bible closes with grace.
meta.theme: The River of Life, the Imminent Return, and the Final Invitation
meta.keyWordMeaning: I am coming (erchomai) — Christ's emphatic, repeated promise (vv. 7, 12, 20) that frames the closing summons and the church's final prayer.
outline: 1-5 The river and tree of life; the servants reign forever
6-9 The angel testifies; John bows and is corrected
10-15 Open the prophecy; final separation of righteous and unrighteous
16-21 The Spirit and Bride say Come; closing warning, blessing, and grace

verses.kjv_text: And he shewed me a pure river of water of life, clear as crystal, proceeding out of the throne of God and of the Lamb.
verses.original_text: Καὶ ἔδειξέν μοι ποταμὸν ὕδατος ζωῆς λαμπρὸν ὡς κρύσταλλον, ἐκπορευόμενον ἐκ τοῦ θρόνου τοῦ Θεοῦ καὶ τοῦ Ἀρνίου.
verses.transliteration: Kai edeixen moi potamon hydatos zōēs lampron hōs krystallon, ekporeuomenon ek tou thronou tou Theou kai tou Arniou.
verses.study_note: The river of Eden (Gen 2:10) and the temple stream of Ezekiel 47 fuse here. ποταμὸν ὕδατος ζωῆς ("river of water of life") flows from the single throne of God and of the Lamb — one throne, two persons.
verses.doctrinal_note: The deity of Christ is woven into the architecture of the new creation: the throne is shared. The Father and the Son together are the source of life that nourishes the redeemed.

verses.kjv_text: In the midst of the street of it, and on either side of the river, was there the tree of life, which bare twelve manner of fruits, and yielded her fruit every month: and the leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations.
verses.original_text: ἐν μέσῳ τῆς πλατείας αὐτῆς καὶ τοῦ ποταμοῦ ἐντεῦθεν καὶ ἐκεῖθεν ξύλον ζωῆς ποιοῦν καρποὺς δώδεκα, κατὰ μῆνα ἕκαστον ἀποδιδοῦν τὸν καρπὸν αὐτοῦ, καὶ τὰ φύλλα τοῦ ξύλου εἰς θεραπείαν τῶν ἐθνῶν.
verses.transliteration: en mesō tēs plateias autēs kai tou potamou enteuthen kai ekeithen xylon zōēs poioun karpous dōdeka, kata mēna hekaston apodidoun ton karpon autou, kai ta phylla tou xylou eis therapeian tōn ethnōn.
verses.study_note: ξύλον ζωῆς (tree of life) — the tree barred to humanity since Eden (Gen 3:24) is now openly accessible. Twelve crops a year, leaves for healing (θεραπεία; cf. Ezek 47:12).
verses.doctrinal_note: What sin closed, the gospel reopens — and amplifies. The new creation is not merely Eden restored; it is Eden surpassed.

verses.kjv_text: And there shall be no more curse: but the throne of God and of the Lamb shall be in it; and his servants shall serve him:
verses.original_text: καὶ πᾶν κατάθεμα οὐκ ἔσται ἔτι. καὶ ὁ θρόνος τοῦ Θεοῦ καὶ τοῦ Ἀρνίου ἐν αὐτῇ ἔσται, καὶ οἱ δοῦλοι αὐτοῦ λατρεύσουσιν αὐτῷ,
verses.transliteration: kai pan katathema ouk estai eti. kai ho thronos tou Theou kai tou Arniou en autē estai, kai hoi douloi autou latreusousin autō,
verses.study_note: κατάθεμα ("anything accursed") is reversed; the curse of Gen 3:17 is undone. λατρεύω is priestly service, the kind given to God alone (cf. Matt 4:10).
verses.doctrinal_note: Heaven is not idle leisure. Worshipful service — the original Edenic vocation — becomes the joyful occupation of the saints forever.

verses.kjv_text: And they shall see his face; and his name shall be in their foreheads.
verses.original_text: καὶ ὄψονται τὸ πρόσωπον αὐτοῦ, καὶ τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τῶν μετώπων αὐτῶν.
verses.transliteration: kai opsontai to prosōpon autou, kai to onoma autou epi tōn metōpōn autōn.
verses.study_note: The beatific vision: ὄψονται τὸ πρόσωπον αὐτοῦ ("they shall see his face") — what was denied to Moses (Ex 33:20) is given to all the redeemed. The seal of His name on their foreheads marks them as His.
verses.doctrinal_note: The face of God, hidden for our protection in this life (1 Tim 6:16), becomes the daily delight of the saints. Sanctification reaches its climax in the unveiled gaze.

verses.kjv_text: And there shall be no night there; and they need no candle, neither light of the sun; for the Lord God giveth them light: and they shall reign for ever and ever.
verses.original_text: καὶ νὺξ οὐκ ἔσται ἔτι, καὶ οὐκ ἔχουσιν χρείαν φωτὸς λύχνου καὶ φωτὸς ἡλίου, ὅτι Κύριος ὁ Θεὸς φωτίσει ἐπ᾽ αὐτούς, καὶ βασιλεύσουσιν εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων.
verses.transliteration: kai nyx ouk estai eti, kai ouk echousin chreian phōtos lychnou kai phōtos hēliou, hoti Kyrios ho Theos phōtisei ep' autous, kai basileusousin eis tous aiōnas tōn aiōnōn.
verses.study_note: Echoes Isa 60:19. The reign of the saints (ἐβασίλευσαν, 20:4) is reaffirmed in unending tense — εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων.
verses.doctrinal_note: The saints' reign is a function of God's reign: shared, derivative, eternal. Glorification fits us not for vacation but for vocation.

verses.kjv_text: And he said unto me, These sayings are faithful and true: and the Lord God of the holy prophets sent his angel to shew unto his servants the things which must shortly be done.
verses.original_text: Καὶ εἶπέν μοι, Οὗτοι οἱ λόγοι πιστοὶ καὶ ἀληθινοί, καὶ ὁ Κύριος, ὁ Θεὸς τῶν πνευμάτων τῶν προφητῶν, ἀπέστειλεν τὸν ἄγγελον αὐτοῦ δεῖξαι τοῖς δούλοις αὐτοῦ ἃ δεῖ γενέσθαι ἐν τάχει.
verses.transliteration: Kai eipen moi, Houtoi hoi logoi pistoi kai alēthinoi, kai ho Kyrios, ho Theos tōn pneumatōn tōn prophētōn, apesteilen ton angelon autou deixai tois doulois autou ha dei genesthai en tachei.
verses.study_note: The prologue (1:1) is echoed in the epilogue: angelic mediation, divine necessity (δεῖ), imminence (ἐν τάχει). πιστοὶ καὶ ἀληθινοί — the same pair used of Christ (3:14; 19:11).
verses.doctrinal_note: Prophecy's reliability rests in the character of God, the "God of the spirits of the prophets." Scripture's authority is rooted in the truthfulness of its Author.

verses.kjv_text: Behold, I come quickly: blessed is he that keepeth the sayings of the prophecy of this book.
verses.original_text: καὶ ἰδοὺ ἔρχομαι ταχύ. μακάριος ὁ τηρῶν τοὺς λόγους τῆς προφητείας τοῦ βιβλίου τούτου.
verses.transliteration: kai idou erchomai tachy. makarios ho tērōn tous logous tēs prophēteias tou bibliou toutou.
verses.study_note: Christ Himself speaks: ἔρχομαι ταχύ ("I am coming quickly"). The sixth beatitude (μακάριος): blessing on those who keep (τηρέω) — not merely read — the prophecy.
verses.doctrinal_note: Prophecy is given for obedience, not curiosity. The right response to Revelation is not speculation but consecration.

verses.kjv_text: And I John saw these things, and heard them. And when I had heard and seen, I fell down to worship before the feet of the angel which shewed me these things.
verses.original_text: Κἀγὼ Ἰωάννης ὁ ἀκούων καὶ βλέπων ταῦτα. καὶ ὅτε ἤκουσα καὶ ἔβλεψα, ἔπεσα προσκυνῆσαι ἔμπροσθεν τῶν ποδῶν τοῦ ἀγγέλου τοῦ δεικνύοντός μοι ταῦτα.
verses.transliteration: Kagō Iōannēs ho akouōn kai blepōn tauta. kai hote ēkousa kai eblepsa, epesa proskynēsai emprosthen tōn podōn tou angelou tou deiknyontos moi tauta.
verses.study_note: John signs the work in his own name (cf. 1:9). His repeated misstep (cf. 19:10) underscores how overwhelming the visions are even to an apostle.
verses.doctrinal_note: Even apostles can confuse messenger with sender when grace blazes too brightly. Revelation is not unmediated mysticism; it is given by Christ, through angels, to the church.

verses.kjv_text: Then saith he unto me, See thou do it not: for I am thy fellowservant, and of thy brethren the prophets, and of them which keep the sayings of this book: worship God.
verses.original_text: καὶ λέγει μοι, Ὅρα μή· σύνδουλός σού εἰμι καὶ τῶν ἀδελφῶν σου τῶν προφητῶν καὶ τῶν τηρούντων τοὺς λόγους τοῦ βιβλίου τούτου· τῷ Θεῷ προσκύνησον.
verses.transliteration: kai legei moi, Hora mē; syndoulos sou eimi kai tōn adelphōn sou tōn prophētōn kai tōn tērountōn tous logous tou bibliou toutou; tō Theō proskynēson.
verses.study_note: σύνδουλος ("fellow-servant") binds angels and saints in one rank: servants of God. προσκύνησον is imperative — "Worship!" — reserved for God alone.
verses.doctrinal_note: Worship is the boundary that no creature, however glorious, may transgress. By contrast, worship is offered freely to the Lamb (5:13), proving His full deity.

verses.kjv_text: And he saith unto me, Seal not the sayings of the prophecy of this book: for the time is at hand.
verses.original_text: καὶ λέγει μοι, Μὴ σφραγίσῃς τοὺς λόγους τῆς προφητείας τοῦ βιβλίου τούτου, ὁ καιρὸς γὰρ ἐγγύς ἐστιν.
verses.transliteration: kai legei moi, Mē sphragisēs tous logous tēs prophēteias tou bibliou toutou, ho kairos gar engys estin.
verses.study_note: Reverses Daniel's instruction to seal his vision (Dan 12:4). Revelation is for the present church, not a distant cipher.
verses.doctrinal_note: The Apocalypse is open Scripture for ordinary saints. Its central claims — judgment is coming, the Lamb is reigning, Christ returns — are meant to be heard and lived now.

verses.kjv_text: He that is unjust, let him be unjust still: and he which is filthy, let him be filthy still: and he that is righteous, let him be righteous still: and he that is holy, let him be holy still.
verses.original_text: ὁ ἀδικῶν ἀδικησάτω ἔτι, καὶ ὁ ῥυπαρὸς ῥυπαρευθήτω ἔτι, καὶ ὁ δίκαιος δικαιοσύνην ποιησάτω ἔτι, καὶ ὁ ἅγιος ἁγιασθήτω ἔτι.
verses.transliteration: ho adikōn adikēsatō eti, kai ho rhyparos rhypareuthētō eti, kai ho dikaios dikaiosynēn poiēsatō eti, kai ho hagios hagiasthētō eti.
verses.study_note: Four imperatives: unjust → keep on; filthy → keep on; righteous → keep on; holy → keep on. The grim parallel to the gracious: judgment seals the trajectory each soul has chosen.
verses.doctrinal_note: When Christ comes, character is fixed. The verses are not divine permission to sin but a sober warning that delay is dangerous and the day of decision is now.

verses.kjv_text: And, behold, I come quickly; and my reward is with me, to give every man according as his work shall be.
verses.original_text: Ἰδοὺ ἔρχομαι ταχύ, καὶ ὁ μισθός μου μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ, ἀποδοῦναι ἑκάστῳ ὡς τὸ ἔργον ἐστὶν αὐτοῦ.
verses.transliteration: Idou erchomai tachy, kai ho misthos mou met' emou, apodounai hekastō hōs to ergon estin autou.
verses.study_note: μισθός is wages, recompense; cf. Isa 40:10; 62:11. Not merit-pay but proportionate recognition: each believer's service is remembered (1 Cor 3:8).
verses.doctrinal_note: Salvation is by grace; rewards are real. Faithfulness counts in the courts of heaven, even when overlooked on earth.

verses.kjv_text: I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, the first and the last.
verses.original_text: ἐγὼ τὸ Ἄλφα καὶ τὸ Ὦ, ὁ πρῶτος καὶ ὁ ἔσχατος, ἡ ἀρχὴ καὶ τὸ τέλος.
verses.transliteration: egō to Alpha kai to Ō, ho prōtos kai ho eschatos, hē archē kai to telos.
verses.study_note: Three titles in one breath. "Alpha and Omega" was the Father's self-description in 1:8; here Christ takes it up as His own. "First and last" was God's title in Isa 44:6; 48:12.
verses.doctrinal_note: The deity of Christ is asserted in the strongest possible terms by the appropriation of titles given exclusively to Yahweh in the Old Testament.

verses.kjv_text: Blessed are they that do his commandments, that they may have right to the tree of life, and may enter in through the gates into the city.
verses.original_text: Μακάριοι οἱ πλύνοντες τὰς στολὰς αὐτῶν, ἵνα ἔσται ἡ ἐξουσία αὐτῶν ἐπὶ τὸ ξύλον τῆς ζωῆς καὶ τοῖς πυλῶσιν εἰσέλθωσιν εἰς τὴν πόλιν.
verses.transliteration: Makarioi hoi plynontes tas stolas autōn, hina estai hē exousia autōn epi to xylon tēs zōēs kai tois pylōsin eiselthōsin eis tēn polin.
verses.study_note: The seventh and final beatitude. The KJV reads "do his commandments"; the older Greek manuscripts read "wash their robes" — an image of the cleansing power of the Lamb's blood (cf. 7:14). Both witness the truth: cleansed lives gain access.
verses.doctrinal_note: The right to the tree of life is granted only to those whose robes Christ has washed. Obedience is the evidence of cleansing; cleansing is the source of access.

verses.kjv_text: For without are dogs, and sorcerers, and whoremongers, and murderers, and idolaters, and whosoever loveth and maketh a lie.
verses.original_text: ἔξω οἱ κύνες καὶ οἱ φάρμακοι καὶ οἱ πόρνοι καὶ οἱ φονεῖς καὶ οἱ εἰδωλολάτραι καὶ πᾶς φιλῶν καὶ ποιῶν ψεῦδος.
verses.transliteration: exō hoi kynes kai hoi pharmakoi kai hoi pornoi kai hoi phoneis kai hoi eidōlolatrai kai pas philōn kai poiōn pseudos.
verses.study_note: "Dogs" — pagan slur for the morally unclean (cf. Phil 3:2; Deut 23:18). Six categories of those barred. The ones who "love" the lie — affection for deception, not merely act.
verses.doctrinal_note: Sin loved becomes sin retained. The decisive question is not whether one sins, but whether one loves the sin or loves the Christ who delivers from it.

verses.kjv_text: I Jesus have sent mine angel to testify unto you these things in the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, and the bright and morning star.
verses.original_text: Ἐγὼ Ἰησοῦς ἔπεμψα τὸν ἄγγελόν μου μαρτυρῆσαι ὑμῖν ταῦτα ἐπὶ ταῖς ἐκκλησίαις. ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ῥίζα καὶ τὸ γένος Δαυίδ, ὁ ἀστὴρ ὁ λαμπρὸς ὁ πρωϊνός.
verses.transliteration: Egō Iēsous epempsa ton angelon mou martyrēsai hymin tauta epi tais ekklēsiais. egō eimi hē rhiza kai to genos Dauid, ho astēr ho lampros ho prōinos.
verses.study_note: Christ names Himself ("I Jesus") and addresses the churches directly. Three messianic titles: root (foundation, cf. Isa 11:1), offspring (descendant), morning star (Num 24:17; 2 Pet 1:19).
verses.doctrinal_note: Jesus is both the source of David's line (root) and its fruit (offspring) — His deity precedes His humanity. The morning star heralds the dawn that ends the long night.

verses.kjv_text: And the Spirit and the bride say, Come. And let him that heareth say, Come. And let him that is athirst come. And whosoever will, let him take the water of life freely.
verses.original_text: Καὶ τὸ Πνεῦμα καὶ ἡ νύμφη λέγουσιν, Ἔρχου. καὶ ὁ ἀκούων εἰπάτω, Ἔρχου. καὶ ὁ διψῶν ἐρχέσθω, ὁ θέλων λαβέτω ὕδωρ ζωῆς δωρεάν.
verses.transliteration: Kai to Pneuma kai hē nymphē legousin, Erchou. kai ho akouōn eipatō, Erchou. kai ho dipsōn erchesthō, ho thelōn labetō hydōr zōēs dōrean.
verses.study_note: Four invitations crescendo. The Spirit and the Bride invite Christ ("Come") and the world (whoever thirsts, "come"). The same verb (ἔρχομαι) for Christ's coming and the sinner's coming.
verses.doctrinal_note: The church's mission is summed in one syllable: "Come." Evangelism is not coercion; it is the offering of free water to the thirsty.

verses.kjv_text: For I testify unto every man that heareth the words of the prophecy of this book, If any man shall add unto these things, God shall add unto him the plagues that are written in this book:
verses.original_text: Μαρτυρῶ ἐγὼ παντὶ τῷ ἀκούοντι τοὺς λόγους τῆς προφητείας τοῦ βιβλίου τούτου· ἐάν τις ἐπιθῇ ἐπ᾽ αὐτά, ἐπιθήσει ὁ Θεὸς ἐπ᾽ αὐτὸν τὰς πληγὰς τὰς γεγραμμένας ἐν τῷ βιβλίῳ τούτῳ.
verses.transliteration: Martyrō egō panti tō akouonti tous logous tēs prophēteias tou bibliou toutou; ean tis epithē ep' auta, epithēsei ho Theos ep' auton tas plēgas tas gegrammenas en tō bibliō toutō.
verses.study_note: A solemn covenant warning (cf. Deut 4:2; 12:32). The verb ἐπιτίθημι ("add upon") matches the punishment to the offense.
verses.doctrinal_note: Scripture is sufficient and finished. Adding to it — by tradition, novel revelation, or speculative gloss — is judged. The closed canon is itself a mercy.

verses.kjv_text: And if any man shall take away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God shall take away his part out of the book of life, and out of the holy city, and from the things which are written in this book.
verses.original_text: καὶ ἐάν τις ἀφέλῃ ἀπὸ τῶν λόγων τοῦ βιβλίου τῆς προφητείας ταύτης, ἀφελεῖ ὁ Θεὸς τὸ μέρος αὐτοῦ ἀπὸ τοῦ ξύλου τῆς ζωῆς καὶ ἐκ τῆς πόλεως τῆς ἁγίας, τῶν γεγραμμένων ἐν τῷ βιβλίῳ τούτῳ.
verses.transliteration: kai ean tis aphelē apo tōn logōn tou bibliou tēs prophēteias tautēs, aphelei ho Theos to meros autou apo tou xylou tēs zōēs kai ek tēs poleōs tēs hagias, tōn gegrammenōn en tō bibliō toutō.
verses.study_note: Paired with v. 18: subtraction is as serious as addition. Loss of share in the tree of life and the holy city — the very promises just unveiled.
verses.doctrinal_note: Scripture cannot be edited to suit the editor. Selective Christianity — keeping comfortable parts and discarding hard ones — risks losing the whole.

verses.kjv_text: He which testifieth these things saith, Surely I come quickly. Amen. Even so, come, Lord Jesus.
verses.original_text: Λέγει ὁ μαρτυρῶν ταῦτα, Ναί, ἔρχομαι ταχύ. Ἀμήν, ἔρχου, Κύριε Ἰησοῦ.
verses.transliteration: Legei ho martyrōn tauta, Nai, erchomai tachy. Amēn, erchou, Kyrie Iēsou.
verses.study_note: Christ's third "I come quickly" (cf. vv. 7, 12) — final, double-affirmed by Ναί ("Yes"). The church's prayer answers: Amen, ἔρχου Κύριε Ἰησοῦ — the Greek equivalent of the early Aramaic prayer Marana tha (1 Cor 16:22).
verses.doctrinal_note: The earliest Christian prayer was for Christ's return. To pray "Maranatha" is to be a true descendant of the apostolic church.

verses.kjv_text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
verses.original_text: Ἡ χάρις τοῦ Κυρίου Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ μετὰ πάντων τῶν ἁγίων. Ἀμήν.
verses.transliteration: Hē charis tou Kyriou Iēsou Christou meta pantōn tōn hagiōn. Amēn.
verses.study_note: Revelation closes as Paul's letters close: with grace. After thunder, trumpets, bowls, beasts, and final judgment, the last note of Scripture is not wrath but χάρις.
verses.doctrinal_note: The Bible begins "In the beginning God…" and ends "…grace…with you all." Between these two brackets, every promise of Scripture is fulfilled.

1.meaning: River — the great life-giving stream of Eden (Gen 2:10) and Ezekiel's temple (Ezek 47); now flowing from God Himself.

2.meaning: Tree, wood — the same word used for the cross (Acts 5:30; 1 Pet 2:24); the tree of life and the tree of death meet in Christ.
Healing, therapy — care that mends; the leaves bring restoration to the nations.

3.meaning: Anything cursed; intensified form of κατάρα. The Edenic curse is undone.
To serve in worship; the priestly word, used only of service rendered to God (Matt 4:10).

4.meaning: Face, presence — what was hidden from Moses (Ex 33:20) is unveiled to the saints. The beatific vision.

6.meaning: Quickly, suddenly — denoting suddenness of arrival rather than nearness of date (cf. 1:1).

7.meaning: To keep, guard, observe — used of Christ keeping the Father's word (John 8:55) and disciples keeping His.

9.meaning: Fellow-servant; angels and saints share one rank under God's lordship.

10.meaning: To seal — Daniel was told to seal his vision until the end (Dan 12:4); John is told to leave his open.

12.meaning: Wages, recompense — what the Lord brings with Him for each believer's faithful labor.

13.meaning: Beginning, origin, first cause — Christ as the source from which all proceeds.
End, goal, consummation — Christ as the destination to which all moves.

14.meaning: Authority, right — the legal entitlement to enter granted to those whose robes are washed.

16.meaning: Star — the morning star heralds the dawn (cf. Num 24:17, the messianic star).

17.meaning: To thirst — used of spiritual longing (Matt 5:6; John 7:37). The qualification for the free water of life.

18.meaning: To add upon, lay on — the verb is used both of the offense (adding to Scripture) and the punishment (adding plagues).

20.meaning: Truly, so be it — the church's ratification of Christ's promise; transliterated from Hebrew.

21.meaning: Grace, unmerited favor — the closing word of the Bible, signaling that the entire story is gospel.
